schools.summary: The making of the altar of incense: 'and thou shalt make an altar to burn incense upon: of shittim wood shalt thou make it' — a second altar, the golden altar of incense (of acacia overlaid with gold, vv.3), distinct from the brazen altar of burnt offering, made for burning incense in the holy place, before the veil (vv.6, the altar of incense, the small golden altar, in the holy place, where the sweet incense was burned morning and evening, vv.7-8); the figure — the altar of incense, where the sweet incense was burned (the witnesses noting the meaning — the incense a figure of prayer, the prayers of the saints, ascending to God as sweet incense, Psalm 141:2, 'let my prayer be set forth before thee as incense; and the lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice'; Revelation 5:8, 'golden vials full of odours, which are the prayers of saints'; Revelation 8:3-4, 'much incense, that he should offer it with the prayers of all saints... and the smoke of the incense, which came with the prayers of the saints, ascended up before God,' the lesson — prayer ascending to God as sweet incense, acceptable, fragrant, and the figure of Christ's intercession, the incense offered with the prayers of the saints, the merit of Christ making the prayers acceptable, the golden altar a figure of Christ through whom our prayers ascend, Hebrews 13:15, the incense the sweet savor of his merit perfuming our prayers); 'of shittim wood' (the acacia, overlaid with gold, vv.3, the incorruptible wood, the figure, as the ark, the humanity of Christ overlaid with the gold of deity, the providence, the golden altar of incense, the place of prayer, the figure of Christ and the prayers of the saints ascending before God) — Henry, Calvin, Wesley, Gill.

debates.question: What does the altar of incense signify?

sides.position: A figure of prayer ascending to God as sweet incense (Psalm 141:2; Revelation 5:8; 8:3-4) - the prayers of the saints made fragrant and acceptable, and of Christ's intercession through whom our prayers ascend (Hebrews 13:15), the incense the sweet savor of his merit

themes: And thou shalt make an altar to burn incense upon: of shittim wood shalt thou make it
the making of the altar of incense (the crux of prayer before God)
the incense a figure of prayer ascending to God (Psalm 141:2; Revelation 5:8; 8:3-4)
made acceptable through Christ's intercession (Hebrews 13:15)

schools.summary: The dimensions and horns of the incense altar: 'a cubit shall be the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be: and two cubits shall be the height thereof: the horns thereof shall be of the same' — the altar of incense a cubit square and two cubits high (small, tall, foursquare), with horns of one piece (as the brazen altar, Exodus 27:2, the dimensions, the small golden altar, the horns, the projections, of the same piece, for the blood of atonement, vv.10); the altar foursquare (the perfect square, the symmetry) — the shape (the small golden altar); 'the horns thereof shall be of the same' (the four horns, of one piece, for the blood of the sin offering, vv.10; Leviticus 4:7, the figure, the horns of the altar of incense, the blood applied, the prayer joined with the atonement, the providence, the golden altar with its horns, the prayer and the atoning blood) — Keil and Delitzsch, Ellicott, Gill.

themes: A cubit shall be the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be: and two cubits shall be the height thereof: the horns thereof shall be of the same
the dimensions and horns of the incense altar
the small golden altar, a cubit square and two cubits high
the horns for the atoning blood (vv.10; Exodus 27:2)

schools.summary: The incense altar overlaid with gold: 'and thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about' — the altar of incense (of acacia) overlaid with pure gold (top, sides, horns), with a golden crown (rim) around it (the gold, the golden altar, distinct from the brazen altar of the court, the crown, the golden rim, the ornament, the figure, the golden altar of prayer, the acacia overlaid with gold, as the ark, the humanity and deity of Christ); the gold overlay (the golden altar) — the glory (the figure, the golden altar of prayer, in the holy place); 'a crown of gold round about' (the golden crown, the rim, the ornament, as the ark and table, the figure, the crown, the glory of the altar of prayer, the providence, the golden altar of incense, the place of prayer, crowned with gold) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold... and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about
the incense altar overlaid with gold
the golden altar of prayer in the holy place
the acacia overlaid with gold, as the ark, crowned with gold

schools.summary: The rings for carrying the incense altar: 'and two golden rings shalt thou make to it under the crown of it, by the two corners thereof, upon the two sides of it shalt thou make it; and they shall be for places for the staves to bear it withal' — two golden rings made under the crown, on the two sides, to receive the staves for carrying (the rings, for the bearing of the altar, the provision for transport, the golden altar borne by staves, as the ark and table, the pilgrim sanctuary); the gold rings (for the staves) — the provision (the carrying); 'for places for the staves to bear it withal' (the rings for the staves, the altar carried by the staves, the providence, the golden altar borne in the journey, the pilgrim sanctuary ever ready to move) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And two golden rings shalt thou make to it under the crown of it... for places for the staves to bear it withal
the rings for carrying the incense altar
the provision for bearing the golden altar by staves
the pilgrim sanctuary ever ready to move

schools.summary: The staves for bearing the incense altar: 'and thou shalt make the staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold' — the staves (of acacia overlaid with gold, matching the golden altar) for carrying the altar of incense (the staves, the poles, gold-overlaid wood, as the ark's, the provision for bearing, the golden altar carried by the staves, the pilgrim sanctuary, the altar ever ready to move); the staves of acacia (for carrying) — the poles (the bearing); 'overlay them with gold' (the gold overlay, matching the golden altar, the provision, the staves through the rings, the altar borne, the providence, the golden altar carried in the journey, the pilgrim sanctuary) — Gill.

themes: And thou shalt make the staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold
the staves for bearing the incense altar
the gold-overlaid poles for carrying the golden altar
the pilgrim sanctuary ever ready to move

schools.summary: The incense altar placed before the veil and the mercy seat: 'and thou shalt put it before the vail that is by the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat that is over the testimony, where I will meet with thee' — the altar of incense placed in the holy place, before the veil (the curtain dividing the holy from the most holy), just outside the most holy place, nearest to the ark and mercy seat (the placement, the altar of prayer nearest the holiest, before the veil, before the mercy seat, the nearness, the prayer offered closest to the presence of God, just before the veil); the altar before the mercy seat (nearest the presence, before the veil) — the figure (prayer offered before the mercy seat, the place of propitiation, the prayer joined with the atonement, ascending before God); 'where I will meet with thee' (the place of God's meeting, the mercy seat, vv.22; Exodus 25:22, the figure, prayer offered before the place of meeting, the prayer ascending where God meets his people in mercy, the access through the propitiation, the providence, the altar of prayer nearest the mercy seat, prayer joined with the atonement, ascending before God) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt put it before the vail that is by the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat that is over the testimony, where I will meet with thee
the incense altar placed before the veil and the mercy seat
prayer offered nearest the holiest, before the place of propitiation
prayer ascending where God meets his people in mercy (Exodus 25:22)

schools.summary: The daily morning incense: 'and Aaron shall burn thereon sweet incense every morning: when he dresseth the lamps, he shall burn incense upon it' — the high priest burning sweet incense on the golden altar every morning, when he dressed (trimmed) the lamps of the candlestick (the daily incense, the perpetual offering of incense, morning and evening, vv.8, the figure, the daily prayer, the continual incense of prayer ascending to God, morning by morning); the sweet incense every morning (the daily offering) — the figure (the morning prayer, the daily, perpetual incense of prayer, the church's continual worship, ascending morning and evening); 'when he dresseth the lamps, he shall burn incense upon it' (the incense burned with the dressing of the lamps, the prayer joined with the tending of the light, the worship and the light together, the figure, prayer and the light of testimony together, the morning worship, the providence, the daily incense, the continual prayer, morning by morning) — Ellicott, Gill, Wesley.

themes: And Aaron shall burn thereon sweet incense every morning: when he dresseth the lamps, he shall burn incense upon it
the daily morning incense
the daily prayer ascending to God, morning by morning
the worship joined with the light of testimony

schools.summary: The perpetual incense morning and evening: 'and when Aaron lighteth the lamps at even, he shall burn incense upon it, a perpetual incense before the LORD throughout your generations' — the high priest burning incense again in the evening, when he lit the lamps, the incense 'perpetual' (continual, morning and evening, in every generation, the perpetual incense, the unceasing prayer, morning and evening, before the LORD, throughout the generations); the evening incense (with the lighting of the lamps) — the daily evening prayer (the figure, the evening worship, the prayer ascending); 'a perpetual incense before the LORD throughout your generations' (the perpetual incense, the unceasing prayer, morning and evening, in every generation, the figure, the continual prayer of the church, the perpetual incense, the prayers of the saints ever ascending, Revelation 8:3-4; 'pray without ceasing,' 1 Thessalonians 5:17, the providence, the perpetual incense, the unceasing prayer before the LORD) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And when Aaron lighteth the lamps at even, he shall burn incense upon it, a perpetual incense before the LORD throughout your generations
the perpetual incense morning and evening
the continual prayer of the church, ever ascending before God
pray without ceasing (Revelation 8:3-4; 1 Thessalonians 5:17)

schools.summary: No strange incense or sacrifice on the golden altar: 'ye shall offer no strange incense thereon, nor burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering; and ye shall not pour drink offering thereon' — no 'strange' (unauthorized) incense, nor any burnt offering, meat offering, or drink offering, to be offered on the golden altar (only the appointed sweet incense, the prohibition, the altar of incense reserved for the appointed incense alone, the warning, no strange fire, no unauthorized worship, Leviticus 10:1-2, Nadab and Abihu and the strange fire); the prohibition of strange incense (unauthorized, not the appointed) — the warning (the worship of God only as he appoints, no will-worship, Leviticus 10:1-2); 'nor burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering; and ye shall not pour drink offering thereon' (the golden altar reserved for the incense alone, no other offering, the figure, the altar of prayer not to be profaned with strange worship, prayer to be offered as God appoints, in purity, the providence, the holy worship guarded, the prayer pure and appointed, no strange fire) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: Ye shall offer no strange incense thereon, nor burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering; and ye shall not pour drink offering thereon
no strange incense or sacrifice on the golden altar
the altar reserved for the appointed incense alone
the worship of God only as he appoints, no strange fire (Leviticus 10:1-2)

schools.summary: The yearly atonement on the horns of the incense altar: 'and Aaron shall make an atonement upon the horns of it once in a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonements: once in the year shall he make atonement upon it throughout your generations: it is most holy unto the LORD' — once a year (on the day of atonement, Leviticus 16:18-19), the high priest putting the blood of the sin offering on the horns of the golden altar, to make atonement for it (the yearly atonement, the cleansing of the altar of incense by the blood, the figure, the altar of prayer itself needing atonement, the prayer cleansed by the blood, even our prayers needing the atonement of Christ); the blood of atonement on the horns (the cleansing of the altar, Leviticus 16:18-19) — the figure (the prayer joined with the atonement, the blood applied even to the altar of prayer, our prayers needing the cleansing blood of Christ, Hebrews 9:14; 1 Peter 2:5, our sacrifices acceptable by Christ); 'once in the year shall he make atonement upon it throughout your generations: it is most holy unto the LORD' (the altar most holy, the golden altar of prayer, hallowed, cleansed by the blood, the providence, the altar of prayer atoned for, most holy, the figure of prayer made holy and acceptable through the blood of Christ) — Calvin, Gill, Ellicott, Wesley.

themes: And Aaron shall make an atonement upon the horns of it once in a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonements... it is most holy unto the LORD
the yearly atonement on the horns of the incense altar
the altar of prayer cleansed by the blood (Leviticus 16:18-19)
a figure of prayer made acceptable through the blood of Christ (Hebrews 9:14)

schools.summary: The LORD beginning the law of the atonement money: 'and the LORD spake unto Moses, saying' — God beginning a new instruction (the law of the ransom, the atonement money, vv.12-16, the divine word, the law of the half-shekel ransom, the providence, the LORD ordaining the ransom for the soul, the atonement money for the service of the sanctuary); the LORD speaking (the new law, the ransom, vv.12) — the divine instruction (the law of atonement); the LORD beginning the law of the ransom money (the half-shekel, vv.12-16) — the atonement for the soul (the providence, the ransom ordained, the figure of the redemption by a price) — Henry, Gill, Keil.

themes: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying
the LORD beginning the law of the atonement money
God ordaining the ransom for the soul (vv.12-16)
the figure of the redemption by a price

schools.summary: The ransom for the soul: 'when thou takest the sum of the children of Israel after their number, then shall they give every man a ransom for his soul unto the LORD, when thou numberest them; that there be no plague among them, when thou numberest them' — the ransom — 'every man a ransom for his soul unto the LORD': when the people were numbered (counted in a census), each man to give a ransom (a price, the half-shekel, vv.13) for his soul (his life), that there be no plague among them (the witnesses noting the meaning — the ransom for the soul, the acknowledgment that the life is forfeit and must be redeemed, that man owes his life to God and cannot be numbered as God's own without a ransom, the lesson — the soul cannot be redeemed by man's own price, 'none can by any means redeem his brother, nor give to God a ransom for him: for the redemption of their soul is precious,' Psalm 49:7-8, but God appoints a ransom, the figure of Christ the true ransom — the redemption of the soul not with silver and gold, the half-shekel a token, but with the precious blood of Christ, 1 Peter 1:18-19, 'ye were not redeemed with corruptible things, as silver and gold... but with the precious blood of Christ,' Christ the ransom, 'the Son of man came... to give his life a ransom for many,' Matthew 20:28; 1 Timothy 2:6, 'who gave himself a ransom for all,' the soul redeemed by the price of Christ); 'that there be no plague among them, when thou numberest them' (the ransom averting the plague, the wrath, that might come on a presumptuous numbering, as later, 2 Samuel 24, David's census and the plague, the ransom the means of safety, the figure, the redemption averting the wrath, the soul ransomed safe); every man a ransom for his soul — the price of the soul, the redemption (the providence, the ransom money, the figure of Christ the ransom, the soul redeemed by his blood) — Calvin, Wesley, JFB, Gill.

debates.question: What does the ransom for the soul signify?

sides.position: The acknowledgment that the forfeit life must be redeemed, and man cannot redeem his own soul (Psalm 49:7-8) - a figure of Christ the true ransom, the soul redeemed not with silver and gold but with his precious blood (1 Peter 1:18-19; Matthew 20:28; 1 Timothy 2:6); the ransom averting the plague, the means of safety

themes: When thou takest the sum of the children of Israel... then shall they give every man a ransom for his soul unto the LORD... that there be no plague among them
the ransom for the soul (the crux of redemption by a price)
the forfeit life redeemed, man cannot redeem his own soul (Psalm 49:7-8)
a figure of Christ the true ransom, the soul redeemed by his precious blood (1 Peter 1:18-19; Matthew 20:28; 1 Timothy 2:6)

schools.summary: The half-shekel ransom: 'this they shall give, every one that passeth among them that are numbered, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary: (a shekel is twenty gerahs:) an half shekel shall be the offering of the LORD' — every numbered person giving a half-shekel (by the standard weight of the sanctuary, twenty gerahs to the shekel) as the offering of the LORD (the half-shekel, the fixed ransom price, the same for all, the offering of the LORD, the atonement money for the service of the sanctuary, vv.16); the half-shekel by the sanctuary standard (the fixed price) — the ransom (the figure, the token price, the half-shekel, pointing to the true ransom in Christ); 'an half shekel shall be the offering of the LORD' (the half-shekel the LORD's offering, the atonement money, for the sanctuary, the providence, the ransom money, the figure of the redemption price, the soul redeemed) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: This they shall give, every one that passeth among them that are numbered, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary... an half shekel shall be the offering of the LORD
the half-shekel ransom
the fixed ransom price, the same for all, the offering of the LORD
the token of the redemption price, the soul redeemed (vv.16)

schools.summary: The offering required from twenty years old: 'every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give an offering unto the LORD' — every man twenty years old and upward (those numbered, of military or adult age) giving the offering (the age, from twenty, the age of adulthood and military service, Numbers 1:3, the offering, the half-shekel, from all the adult men); the age of twenty and above (the numbered, the adults) — the offering required of all of age; 'shall give an offering unto the LORD' (the offering, the half-shekel, the ransom, from all the adult men, the providence, the ransom required of all of age, the redemption price for each soul) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: Every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give an offering unto the LORD
the offering required from twenty years old
the half-shekel from all the adult men (Numbers 1:3)
the redemption price required of each soul

schools.summary: The rich and poor giving the same ransom: 'the rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when they give an offering unto the LORD, to make an atonement for your souls' — the ransom the same for all, rich and poor alike, half a shekel each, to make atonement for their souls (the equality, the same price for every soul, rich or poor, the figure, every soul of equal value in the matter of redemption, the same price for all); the equality (the same ransom for all) — the lesson (every soul equally valued and equally redeemed, the same price, the redemption in Christ the same for all, no soul redeemed for more or less, the one ransom for all, 'there is no difference,' Romans 3:22-24, the rich and poor alike redeemed by the one price of Christ); 'to make an atonement for your souls' (the ransom for atonement, the redemption of the soul, the figure, the one atonement for all souls, the precious blood of Christ the same ransom for rich and poor, Galatians 3:28, the providence, the equal ransom, the one redemption price, the figure of Christ the one ransom for all) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: The rich shall not give more, and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel, when they give an offering unto the LORD, to make an atonement for your souls
the rich and poor giving the same ransom
every soul equally valued and equally redeemed by the one price (Romans 3:22-24)
a figure of Christ the one ransom for all, rich and poor alike (Galatians 3:28)

schools.summary: The atonement money for the service and memorial: 'and thou shalt take the atonement money of the children of Israel, and shalt appoint it for the service of the tabernacle of the congregation; that it may be a memorial unto the children of Israel before the LORD, to make an atonement for your souls' — the ransom money used for the service of the tabernacle (the silver of the sockets and the sanctuary, Exodus 38:25-28), and a memorial for the people, to make atonement for their souls (the atonement money, the ransom applied to the sanctuary's service, the memorial, the people remembered before God, redeemed); the money for the service of the tabernacle (the silver, the sockets, Exodus 38:25-28) — the use (the ransom applied to God's house); 'that it may be a memorial unto the children of Israel before the LORD, to make an atonement for your souls' (the money a memorial, the people remembered before God, redeemed, the atonement, the soul ransomed, the figure, the redemption remembered, the people atoned for, the figure of Christ's ransom, by which the redeemed are remembered before God, the providence, the atonement money, the redemption price, the people remembered and atoned for) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt take the atonement money of the children of Israel, and shalt appoint it for the service of the tabernacle of the congregation; that it may be a memorial... to make an atonement for your souls
the atonement money for the service and memorial
the ransom applied to God's house (the silver of the sockets, Exodus 38:25-28)
the people remembered and atoned for, the figure of Christ's redemption

schools.summary: The LORD beginning the law of the laver: 'and the LORD spake unto Moses, saying' — God beginning a new instruction (the law of the laver, the washing, vv.18-21, the divine word, the law of the brazen laver, the providence, the LORD ordaining the laver for the washing of the priests, the cleansing required in God's service); the LORD speaking (the new law, the laver, vv.18) — the divine instruction (the law of washing); the LORD beginning the law of the laver (the washing, vv.18-21) — the cleansing of the priests (the providence, the laver ordained, the figure of the washing of regeneration and sanctification) — Henry, Gill, Keil.

themes: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying
the LORD beginning the law of the laver
God ordaining the laver for the washing of the priests (vv.18-21)
the cleansing required in God's service

schools.summary: The laver of brass for washing: 'thou shalt also make a laver of brass, and his foot also of brass, to wash withal: and thou shalt put it between the tabernacle of the congregation and the altar, and thou shalt put water therein' — a brazen laver (basin, with its base) made for washing, set between the altar of burnt offering and the tabernacle, filled with water (the laver, the basin of water for the priests to wash their hands and feet, vv.19-21, the placement, between the altar and the tabernacle, on the way from the altar to the holy place); the figure — the laver for washing, that the priests might wash before ministering (the witnesses noting the lesson — the washing a figure of cleansing, the priests, having offered at the altar of blood, washed at the laver before entering the holy place, the figure of the cleansing required to approach God, the figure of the washing of regeneration, the new birth, the laver of regeneration, Titus 3:5, 'the washing of regeneration,' and of sanctification, the daily cleansing, the washing of the feet, John 13:10, 'he that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet,' the cleansing by the word and the Spirit, Ephesians 5:26, 'that he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word,' the laver, after the altar — the cleansing following the atonement, the washing after the blood, the order of salvation, justified by the blood, sanctified by the washing); 'between the tabernacle of the congregation and the altar' (the laver between the altar and the holy place, the washing after the altar, before entering, the figure, the cleansing after the atonement, the order of the blood then the washing, the providence, the laver of washing, the figure of regeneration and sanctification, the cleansing required to draw near) — Keil and Delitzsch, Wesley, Gill.

debates.question: What does the laver of washing signify?

sides.position: The cleansing required to approach God, a figure of the washing of regeneration (Titus 3:5) and of sanctification (John 13:10; Ephesians 5:26) - the laver after the altar, the cleansing following the atonement, the washing after the blood (justified by the blood, sanctified by the washing)

themes: Thou shalt also make a laver of brass, and his foot also of brass, to wash withal... between the tabernacle of the congregation and the altar
the laver of brass for washing (the crux of the cleansing required)
the cleansing required to approach God, a figure of regeneration (Titus 3:5)
and of sanctification (John 13:10; Ephesians 5:26), the washing after the blood

schools.summary: The priests washing their hands and feet: 'for Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet thereat' — Aaron and his sons washing their hands and feet at the laver before ministering (the washing, the hands, the instruments of work, and the feet, the members of walking, cleansed, the figure, the cleansing of the work and the walk, the hands and feet, the deeds and the conduct, washed before God's service); the washing of hands and feet (the priests cleansed before ministering) — the figure (the hands, the works, and the feet, the walk, cleansed, the daily cleansing of the believer's deeds and conduct, the washing of the feet, John 13:10); the hands and feet washed — the cleansing of the work and the walk (the providence, the priests cleansed before God's service, the figure of the daily sanctification, the cleansing of the deeds and the conduct) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: For Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet thereat
the priests washing their hands and feet
the cleansing of the work (hands) and the walk (feet)
a figure of daily sanctification, the cleansing of deeds and conduct (John 13:10)

schools.summary: The washing required on pain of death: 'when they go into the tabernacle of the congregation, they shall wash with water, that they die not; or when they come near to the altar to minister, to burn offering made by fire unto the LORD' — the priests to wash whenever they entered the tabernacle or came to the altar to minister, on pain of death (the requirement, the washing necessary before ministering, the unwashed priest to die, the holiness, the cleansing required to approach God, the unclean not to draw near); the washing on pain of death (the unwashed priest to die) — the holiness (the cleansing required, the careless approach a deadly sin, the figure, the need of cleansing to approach God, the unclean not to draw near, the necessity of sanctification); 'when they come near to the altar to minister, to burn offering made by fire unto the LORD' (the washing required for all ministry, at the altar and in the holy place, the providence, the cleansing required for God's service, the figure of the sanctification needed to serve and approach God, Hebrews 12:14, 'without holiness no man shall see the Lord') — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: When they go into the tabernacle of the congregation, they shall wash with water, that they die not; or when they come near to the altar to minister
the washing required on pain of death
the cleansing required to approach and serve God, the unclean not to draw near
the sanctification without which none may draw near (Hebrews 12:14)

schools.summary: The perpetual statute of the washing: 'so they shall wash their hands and their feet, that they die not: and it shall be a statute for ever to them, even to him and to his seed throughout their generations' — the washing of hands and feet a perpetual statute (for all generations of priests), on pain of death (the perpetuity, the cleansing required in every generation, the figure, the abiding need of cleansing to approach God, the daily sanctification, in every age); the washing on pain of death (the perpetual requirement) — the cleansing (the holiness); 'it shall be a statute for ever to them, even to him and to his seed throughout their generations' (the perpetual statute, the washing for all generations, the figure, the abiding need of cleansing, the daily sanctification, the figure fulfilled in Christ who cleanses his people, Ephesians 5:26, the perpetual washing of the feet, John 13:10, the providence, the cleansing required in every generation, the figure of the abiding sanctification) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: So they shall wash their hands and their feet, that they die not: and it shall be a statute for ever to them, even to him and to his seed throughout their generations
the perpetual statute of the washing
the cleansing required in every generation, the abiding sanctification
fulfilled in Christ who cleanses his people (Ephesians 5:26; John 13:10)

schools.summary: The LORD beginning the law of the anointing oil: 'moreover the LORD spake unto Moses, saying' — God beginning a new instruction (the law of the holy anointing oil, vv.23-33, the divine word, the law of the anointing oil and the incense, the providence, the LORD ordaining the holy oil for the consecration of the priests and the holy things); the LORD speaking (the new law, the anointing oil, vv.23) — the divine instruction (the law of the holy oil); the LORD beginning the law of the anointing oil (the holy oil, vv.23-33) — the consecration of the priests and the sanctuary (the providence, the holy oil ordained, the figure of the Spirit's anointing) — Henry, Gill.

themes: Moreover the LORD spake unto Moses, saying
the LORD beginning the law of the anointing oil
God ordaining the holy oil for the consecration of the priests and the sanctuary (vv.23-33)
the figure of the Spirit's anointing

schools.summary: The principal spices of the anointing oil: 'take thou also unto thee principal spices, of pure myrrh five hundred shekels, and of sweet cinnamon half so much, even two hundred and fifty shekels, and of sweet calamus two hundred and fifty shekels' — the holy anointing oil made of the chief (principal, finest) spices: pure myrrh, sweet cinnamon, sweet calamus (and cassia, vv.24), in measured quantities, with olive oil (vv.24-25, the spices, the costly, fragrant spices, the finest, the figure, the sweet spices, the fragrance of the Spirit's gifts and graces, the precious ointment); the myrrh, cinnamon, calamus (the fragrant spices, the finest) — the figure (the sweet spices, the graces of the Spirit, the fragrance of Christ's anointing, the precious ointment, Psalm 133:2, 'the precious ointment upon the head... that ran down upon the beard, even Aaron's beard'); the principal spices — the finest, fragrant spices (the providence, the costly spices, the figure of the Spirit's manifold gifts and graces, the fragrance of the anointing, the precious ointment of consecration) — Calvin, Gill, Ellicott, Wesley.

themes: Take thou also unto thee principal spices, of pure myrrh five hundred shekels, and of sweet cinnamon half so much... and of sweet calamus two hundred and fifty shekels
the principal spices of the anointing oil
the finest, fragrant spices - myrrh, cinnamon, calamus
the figure of the Spirit's manifold gifts and the fragrance of the anointing (Psalm 133:2)

schools.summary: The cassia and olive oil of the anointing oil: 'and of cassia five hundred shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary, and of oil olive an hin' — the fourth spice, cassia (five hundred shekels), and a hin of olive oil, completing the holy anointing oil (with the myrrh, cinnamon, calamus, vv.23, the cassia, the fourth fragrant spice, the olive oil, the base of the ointment, the hin of oil into which the spices were compounded); the cassia (the fourth spice, five hundred shekels) — the fragrant spice (the figure, the graces of the Spirit); 'and of oil olive an hin' (the olive oil, the base, the hin, into which the spices were compounded, the figure, the oil the figure of the Spirit, the spices the graces compounded in the oil, the anointing oil the figure of the Spirit's anointing, 1 John 2:20, 27, the providence, the oil and the spices, the holy anointing oil, the figure of the Spirit and his gifts) — JFB, Gill.

themes: And of cassia five hundred shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary, and of oil olive an hin
the cassia and olive oil of the anointing oil
the spices compounded in the olive oil base
the figure of the Spirit and his graces (1 John 2:20)

schools.summary: The holy anointing oil: 'and thou shalt make it an oil of holy ointment, an ointment compound after the art of the apothecary: it shall be an holy anointing oil' — the spices and oil compounded (by the apothecary's art, skillfully blended) into a holy anointing oil (the ointment, the precious, fragrant, compounded oil, the holy anointing oil, for consecrating the priests and the holy things, vv.26-30); the figure — the holy anointing oil (the witnesses noting the meaning — the oil a figure of the Holy Spirit, the anointing of the Spirit, by which the priests and the sanctuary were consecrated, the figure of the Spirit's consecrating, sanctifying anointing, the lesson — the anointing of the Spirit, the believer anointed with the Spirit, 1 John 2:20, 27, 'ye have an unction from the Holy One... the anointing which ye have received of him abideth in you'; 2 Corinthians 1:21-22, 'he which... hath anointed us, is God; who hath also sealed us, and given the earnest of the Spirit,' and the figure of Christ the Anointed, the Messiah, the Christ, 'the Anointed One,' anointed with the Spirit without measure, John 3:34; Acts 10:38, 'God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Ghost and with power'; Psalm 45:7, the oil of gladness above his fellows, Hebrews 1:9, from Christ the Head the anointing flows to his members, Psalm 133:2, the ointment on Aaron's head running down to the skirts, the consecration by the Spirit); 'an ointment compound after the art of the apothecary' (the spices skillfully compounded in the oil, the manifold graces in the one Spirit, the figure, the manifold gifts and graces of the one Spirit, the fragrance of the anointing, the providence, the holy anointing oil, the figure of the Spirit's anointing of Christ and his people) — Calvin, Wesley, Gill.

debates.question: What does the holy anointing oil signify?

sides.position: A figure of the Holy Spirit, the anointing by which the priests and sanctuary were consecrated - the figure of the believer's anointing with the Spirit (1 John 2:20, 27; 2 Corinthians 1:21-22) and of Christ the Anointed, anointed with the Spirit without measure (Acts 10:38; John 3:34; Hebrews 1:9), from whom the anointing flows to his members (Psalm 133:2)

themes: And thou shalt make it an oil of holy ointment, an ointment compound after the art of the apothecary: it shall be an holy anointing oil
the holy anointing oil (the crux of the Spirit's anointing)
a figure of the Holy Spirit, the anointing of Christ the Anointed (Acts 10:38; John 3:34)
and of his people (1 John 2:20, 27; 2 Corinthians 1:21-22), flowing from the Head to his members (Psalm 133:2)

schools.summary: The anointing of the tabernacle and ark: 'and thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of the congregation therewith, and the ark of the testimony' — the holy oil used to anoint (consecrate, set apart) the tabernacle and the ark of the testimony (and all the furniture, vv.27-28, the anointing, the consecration of the sanctuary and its furniture by the oil, the setting apart, the holy things hallowed by the anointing); the anointing of the tabernacle (the consecration of the holy place) — the setting apart (the hallowing); 'and the ark of the testimony' (the ark anointed, the holiest object, consecrated, the figure, the sanctuary and the holy things consecrated by the oil, the figure of the Spirit consecrating, the things of God set apart by the Spirit's anointing, the providence, the holy things hallowed by the anointing oil, the figure of the Spirit's consecration) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of the congregation therewith, and the ark of the testimony
the anointing of the tabernacle and ark
the sanctuary and its furniture hallowed by the oil
the figure of the Spirit's consecration

schools.summary: The anointing of the table, candlestick, and incense altar: 'and the table and all his vessels, and the candlestick and his vessels, and the altar of incense' — the table of shewbread, the golden candlestick, and the altar of incense (with their vessels) also anointed (the furniture of the holy place consecrated, the anointing, all the holy furniture set apart by the oil, the figure, the holy things of the sanctuary consecrated by the Spirit); the table and candlestick (and their vessels) anointed — the consecration (the hallowing); 'and the altar of incense' (the golden altar anointed, consecrated, the figure, all the holy furniture consecrated by the oil, the figure of the Spirit consecrating the things and the worship of God, the providence, the holy things hallowed by the anointing) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And the table and all his vessels, and the candlestick and his vessels, and the altar of incense
the anointing of the table, candlestick, and incense altar
all the holy furniture set apart by the oil
the figure of the Spirit's consecration

schools.summary: The anointing of the altar and laver: 'and the altar of burnt offering with all his vessels, and the laver and his foot' — the brazen altar of burnt offering (with its vessels) and the laver (with its base) also anointed (the furniture of the court consecrated, the anointing, the altar and laver set apart by the oil, the figure, all the holy things, in the court and the holy place, consecrated by the Spirit); the altar of burnt offering (and its vessels) anointed — the consecration (the hallowing); 'and the laver and his foot' (the laver anointed, consecrated, the figure, all the holy things, even the altar and laver, consecrated by the oil, the figure of the Spirit consecrating the whole service of God, the providence, the holy things hallowed by the anointing) — Gill.

themes: And the altar of burnt offering with all his vessels, and the laver and his foot
the anointing of the altar and laver
the holy things of the court set apart by the oil
the figure of the Spirit's consecration

schools.summary: The holy things made most holy by the anointing: 'and thou shalt sanctify them, that they may be most holy: whatsoever toucheth them shall be holy' — the anointing sanctifying (setting apart, making holy) the furniture, that they might be most holy, so that whatever touched them became holy (the sanctifying, the holy things made most holy by the oil, the contagion of holiness, whatever touched them holy, the holiness communicated, cf. Matthew 23:19, 'the altar that sanctifieth the gift'); the sanctifying (the furniture made most holy by the anointing) — the consecration (the holiness); 'whatsoever toucheth them shall be holy' (the holiness communicated, whatever touched the most holy things became holy, the figure, the holiness of the consecrated things, the holiness imparted, the figure of the holiness of Christ and his sanctifying of his people, all that touch him made holy, the providence, the holy things most holy, the holiness communicated, the figure of the sanctifying of all that is in Christ) — Gill.

themes: And thou shalt sanctify them, that they may be most holy: whatsoever toucheth them shall be holy
the holy things made most holy by the anointing
the holiness communicated, whatever touched them made holy (Matthew 23:19)
a figure of Christ's sanctifying of his people

schools.summary: The anointing and consecration of the priests with the holy oil: 'and thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them, that they may minister unto me in the priest's office' — Aaron and his sons anointed with the holy oil and consecrated to the priesthood (the anointing of the priests, the consecration by the oil, the setting apart for the office, the figure, the priests anointed with the oil, the figure of the Spirit's anointing of God's servants and people); the priests anointed (with the holy oil, the consecration) — the figure (the anointing of the Spirit, the figure of Christ the Anointed and his anointed people, 1 John 2:20, 27, the high priest anointed, Psalm 133:2); 'consecrate them, that they may minister unto me in the priest's office' (the priests consecrated, set apart for God's service, ch. 29, the figure, the consecration by the Spirit, the priests anointed and set apart, the figure of the believer anointed and consecrated to God's service, a royal priesthood, 1 Peter 2:5, 9, the providence, the priests anointed and consecrated, the figure of the Spirit's anointing) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them, that they may minister unto me in the priest's office
the anointing and consecration of the priests with the holy oil
the priests anointed and set apart for God's service (ch. 29)
a figure of the Spirit's anointing of Christ and his priestly people (1 John 2:20; 1 Peter 2:5)

schools.summary: The holy oil set apart to the LORD for ever: 'and thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, This shall be an holy anointing oil unto me throughout your generations' — the oil declared a holy anointing oil unto the LORD, throughout all generations (reserved for God's use alone, the consecration of the priests and holy things, the holiness, the oil set apart, sacred, not for common use, the perpetuity, the holy oil for all generations); the oil holy unto the LORD (reserved for sacred use) — the holiness (the consecration to God); 'throughout your generations' (the holy oil for all generations, the abiding consecration, the figure, the Spirit's anointing, holy, sacred, for God's people in every age, the providence, the holy oil reserved to God, the figure of the Spirit's consecrating anointing) — Gill.

themes: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, This shall be an holy anointing oil unto me throughout your generations
the holy oil set apart to the LORD for ever
the oil reserved to God, sacred, not for common use
the figure of the Spirit's consecrating anointing

schools.summary: The holy oil not to be imitated or put to common use: 'upon man's flesh shall it not be poured, neither shall ye make any other like it, after the composition of it: it is holy, and it shall be holy unto you' — the holy oil not to be poured on common men (for ordinary use), nor imitated (no like oil compounded for common use, the prohibition, the oil reserved for the sacred consecration alone, not common or counterfeit, the holiness, the oil holy, set apart, not to be profaned or imitated); the oil not for common men (not for ordinary anointing) — the reservation (the holiness); 'neither shall ye make any other like it, after the composition of it: it is holy' (no imitation, no counterfeit holy oil, the figure, the Spirit's anointing not to be counterfeited or profaned, the holy things of God not to be imitated or put to common use, the warning against profaning the sacred, against the counterfeit anointing, the providence, the holy oil sacred, not to be imitated, the figure of the true and unimitated anointing of the Spirit) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: Upon man's flesh shall it not be poured, neither shall ye make any other like it, after the composition of it: it is holy, and it shall be holy unto you
the holy oil not to be imitated or put to common use
the sacred oil reserved, not common or counterfeit
the figure of the true and unprofaned anointing of the Spirit

schools.summary: The penalty for imitating or profaning the holy oil: 'whosoever compoundeth any like it, or whosoever putteth any of it upon a stranger, shall even be cut off from his people' — whoever made a like oil (imitated the holy composition) or put it on a 'stranger' (one not consecrated, a common person) to be cut off from the people (the penalty, excision, the cutting-off, the severe punishment for profaning the holy, the holiness guarded, the sacred not to be imitated or profaned, on pain of cutting-off); the imitating or profaning of the oil (making a like oil, or using it on the unconsecrated) — the profanation (the sin against the holy); 'shall even be cut off from his people' (the cutting-off, excommunication, or death, the severe penalty, the figure, the profaning of the sacred, the counterfeiting of the holy, severely judged, the warning against profaning the things of God and the anointing of the Spirit, Acts 8:18-23, Simon Magus and the gift of God, the providence, the holiness guarded, the profaner cut off, the figure of the judgment on those who profane the sacred) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: Whosoever compoundeth any like it, or whosoever putteth any of it upon a stranger, shall even be cut off from his people
the penalty for imitating or profaning the holy oil
the severe judgment on profaning or counterfeiting the sacred
the figure of the judgment on those who profane the anointing of the Spirit (Acts 8:18-23)

schools.summary: The holy incense of sweet spices: 'and the LORD said unto Moses, Take unto thee sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum; these sweet spices with pure frankincense: of each shall there be a like weight' — the holy incense made of sweet spices: stacte, onycha, galbanum, and pure frankincense, in equal weights (the incense, the sweet, fragrant compound, for the golden altar, vv.36, the figure, the sweet incense a figure of prayer, the fragrance ascending to God, Psalm 141:2; Revelation 8:3-4); the four ingredients (stacte, onycha, galbanum, frankincense) — the figure (the sweet spices compounded, the manifold graces in prayer, the fragrance of prayer, the witnesses note even galbanum, of itself less pleasant, compounded with the sweet, perhaps the figure that all, even the weaker graces, are needful in the whole); 'of each shall there be a like weight' (the equal weights, the balanced compound, the figure, the manifold graces in due proportion, the balanced fragrance of prayer, the providence, the holy incense, the figure of prayer ascending to God as sweet fragrance) — Calvin, Gill, Ellicott, Wesley.

themes: And the LORD said unto Moses, Take unto thee sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum; these sweet spices with pure frankincense: of each shall there be a like weight
the holy incense of sweet spices
the sweet, fragrant compound for the golden altar
the figure of prayer ascending to God as fragrance (Psalm 141:2; Revelation 8:3-4)

schools.summary: The incense compounded pure and holy: 'and thou shalt make it a perfume, a confection after the art of the apothecary, tempered together, pure and holy' — the sweet spices compounded (by the apothecary's art, skillfully) into a perfume (incense), tempered (mixed, perhaps with salt, vv.35) together, pure and holy (the confection, the compounded incense, the purity and holiness, the incense pure and holy, for the sacred use, the figure, the pure and holy prayer, the fragrance of acceptable worship); the incense compounded (skillfully, tempered, mixed) — the figure (the manifold graces in prayer, the compounded fragrance); 'pure and holy' (the incense pure and holy, sacred, for the holy use, the figure, the pure and holy prayer, the fragrance of acceptable worship, the prayer made pure and holy through Christ, the providence, the holy incense, the figure of pure and acceptable prayer) — Keil and Delitzsch, Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt make it a perfume, a confection after the art of the apothecary, tempered together, pure and holy
the incense compounded pure and holy
the compounded, tempered incense, sacred for the holy use
the figure of pure and holy prayer, the fragrance of acceptable worship

schools.summary: The incense set before the testimony where God meets his people: 'and thou shalt beat some of it very small, and put of it before the testimony in the tabernacle of the congregation, where I will meet with thee: it shall be unto you most holy' — the incense beaten very small (fine, powdered) and put before the testimony (the ark, by the altar of incense), where God met his people (the beaten small, the incense finely powdered, perhaps the figure of the contrite, broken heart in prayer, Psalm 51:17, the placement, before the testimony, the altar of incense, the prayer offered before God's presence); the incense before the testimony (the prayer offered before the place of God's meeting, the mercy seat) — the figure (prayer offered before God, where he meets his people in mercy, the prayer ascending before the presence); 'it shall be unto you most holy' (the incense most holy, sacred, for the holy use, the figure, the most holy prayer, the fragrance of acceptable worship before God, the providence, the incense most holy, before the testimony, the prayer ascending where God meets his people) — Keil and Delitzsch, JFB, Gill.

themes: And thou shalt beat some of it very small, and put of it before the testimony in the tabernacle of the congregation, where I will meet with thee: it shall be unto you most holy
the incense set before the testimony where God meets his people
the incense beaten small, the figure of the contrite heart in prayer (Psalm 51:17)
prayer offered before God, where he meets his people in mercy

schools.summary: The holy incense not to be imitated: 'and as for the perfume which thou shalt make, ye shall not make to yourselves according to the composition thereof: it shall be unto thee holy for the LORD' — the holy incense not to be imitated (no like perfume compounded for common use, the incense holy for the LORD, reserved for the sacred use, not common, the prohibition, the sacred incense not to be counterfeited or profaned, the holiness, the incense holy, set apart for God); the prohibition of imitation (no like incense for common use) — the holiness guarded (the sacred not to be counterfeited); 'it shall be unto thee holy for the LORD' (the incense holy for the LORD, reserved, sacred, the figure, the prayer and worship of God not to be counterfeited or profaned, the holy things reserved to God, the providence, the holy incense reserved, the figure of the true and unprofaned worship of God) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: And as for the perfume which thou shalt make, ye shall not make to yourselves according to the composition thereof: it shall be unto thee holy for the LORD
the holy incense not to be imitated
the sacred incense reserved to God, not common or counterfeit
the figure of the true and unprofaned worship of God

schools.summary: The penalty for imitating the holy incense: 'whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall even be cut off from his people' — whoever made a like incense (imitated the holy composition) for his own use (to smell, to enjoy the fragrance) to be cut off from the people (the penalty, excision, the cutting-off, for profaning the holy by imitation, the holiness guarded, the sacred incense not to be counterfeited or put to common use, on pain of cutting-off); the imitating of the incense (for common enjoyment) — the profanation (the sin against the holy); 'shall even be cut off from his people' (the cutting-off, the severe penalty, the figure, the profaning of the sacred, the counterfeiting of the holy worship, severely judged, the warning against profaning the things and worship of God, the providence, the holiness guarded, the profaner cut off, the figure of the judgment on those who profane the sacred worship) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: Whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall even be cut off from his people
the penalty for imitating the holy incense
the severe judgment on profaning or counterfeiting the sacred worship
the figure of the judgment on those who profane the things of God
